items.csv.description: Please upload a CSV or TSV file.
items.csv.encoding_info: Header rows are detected automatically. UTF-8 and Shift-JIS encoding are supported.
items.csv.store_note: * Selecting a store displays its linked suppliers in the sample
items.csv.required_note: ★ Required: Item name and Supplier. Supplier names must exactly match those registered in the system.
items.csv.matching_note: Column names can differ — you can match them on the next screen.

help.title: Help / Manual

help.index.title: Kurajika CMS Help
help.index.intro: A guide for users new to the CMS. No accounting expertise or technical jargon required — just the operations needed for daily work and initial setup, explained step by step.
help.index.callout_tip: 💡 Jump to any chapter from the menu on the left. We recommend starting with "Screen Map".
help.index.section_intro_desc: The big picture. What each screen is for.
help.index.section_concepts_desc: Clear up common confusion — e.g. stock count vs. ordering.
help.index.section_setup_desc: Initial setup steps for company and store admins.
help.index.language_hint: 🌐 Use the language selector at the top-right to switch to English — also accessible to foreign staff.
help.nav_map.title: Screen Map
help.nav_map.intro: CMS screens are divided into three groups by purpose. Understanding the grouping helps you quickly find the right screen.
help.nav_map.three_groups_intro: The typical order of use follows the groups: ① set up your "materials", ② record daily activity, ③ review the results.
help.nav_map.flow_intro: Example workflow for a new operator.
help.nav_map.flow_step1: When a delivery arrives, enter the invoice in the "Purchase Entry" screen.
help.nav_map.flow_step2: In the morning or after close, count inventory in "Inventory Count" (the SP version works on mobile).
help.nav_map.flow_step3: Open "Order Support" to find items running low, then place orders with suppliers.
help.nav_map.flow_step4: Weekly or monthly, open the Dashboard to check purchase trends.
help.nav_map.flow_step5: Drill into reports to investigate specific items or suppliers.
help.nav_map.tip_next: Next chapter to deepen your understanding:
help.count_vs_order.title: Stock Count vs. Ordering
help.count_vs_order.intro: The most common source of confusion for new users is the difference between the Inventory Count and Order Support screens. Both list the same items, but the ordering and purpose are different.
help.count_vs_order.count_groupedby: By storage location (temp zone → area → shelf)
help.count_vs_order.count_question: "How many are on this shelf, right now?"
help.count_vs_order.order_question: "What should I order from this vendor next?"
help.count_vs_order.same_item: The same item appears on both screens
help.count_vs_order.same_item_intro: Example: an egg case. On Inventory Count it appears under "Fridge / Middle shelf"; on Order Support it appears under the supplier "Torikiyo". Same item, different angle.
help.count_vs_order.screenshot_diagram: Comparison: Count (by location) vs. Order Support (by supplier)
help.count_vs_order.use_count_2: Counting the fridge / shelves in physical order
help.count_vs_order.use_order_1: Just before placing an order with a supplier, to confirm quantities
help.count_vs_order.use_order_2: To see "which supplier has which items running low" at a glance
help.count_vs_order.use_order_3: To check delivery dates and order deadlines
help.count_vs_order.summary: Inventory Count is for "counting on site"; Order Support is for "asking your supplier".
help.count_vs_order.tip_1: For inventory counting, the Mobile (SP) version is handy — filter by purchase frequency to focus on fast-moving items.
help.count_vs_order.tip_2: Order Support has a "Sheet View" toggle — a flat, sortable spreadsheet sorted by frequency by default.
help.count_vs_order.tip_3: Both screens show the "Last Count Date" per item so you notice stale data.
help.setup_shelves.title: Shelves, Temperature Zones & Areas
help.setup_shelves.intro: To make inventory counting simple and accurate, align your in-CMS shelf layout with the real storage locations. Setup takes a bit of time once, but daily counts get dramatically easier afterward.
help.setup_shelves.why_body: When shelves are configured, the Inventory Count screen sorts items in physical storage order.
help.setup_shelves.why_1: Fast and confident counting (everyone follows the same path)
help.setup_shelves.why_2: Fewer missed items (the screen groups them by shelf)
help.setup_shelves.why_3: Shorter onboarding (new staff just walk the order on screen)
help.setup_shelves.three_layers_intro: The CMS manages storage in three nested layers: Temperature Zone → Area → Shelf.
help.setup_shelves.layer1_desc: Frozen / Chilled / Ambient etc. — top-level storage condition.
help.setup_shelves.layer3_desc: Specific shelf within an area — "Top", "Middle", "Bottom" etc.
help.setup_shelves.tip_hierarchy: 💡 The setup screen dynamically narrows the choices: pick a temperature zone → areas filter → shelves filter.
help.setup_shelves.step1_body: Start by registering the temperature zones used in your store.
help.setup_shelves.step1_b: Click Edit on the target store, then open the "Temp Zones" tab.
help.setup_shelves.step1_c: Click "Add", enter a zone name (e.g. "Chilled"), and drag to reorder.
help.setup_shelves.step2_a: In the same store edit screen, open the "Areas" tab.
help.setup_shelves.step2_b: Enter an area name (e.g. "Kitchen Fridge") and select the linked temperature zone.
help.setup_shelves.step2_c: Order areas by the physical walking path (entrance → back of the kitchen) for efficient counting.
help.setup_shelves.step3_body: Finally, register individual shelves (the lowest level).
help.setup_shelves.step3_b: Enter a shelf code (e.g. "A-01") and name (e.g. "Top"), and select the parent area.
help.setup_shelves.step3_c: Put matching labels on the actual shelves so staff don't get confused.
help.setup_shelves.step4_title: ④ Assign Each Item to a Shelf
help.setup_shelves.step4_body: Once shelves are configured, assign each item to its shelf.
help.setup_shelves.step4_b: In the item edit screen, pick temperature zone → area → shelf.
help.setup_shelves.step4_c: After saving, the Inventory Count screen reorders items accordingly.
help.setup_shelves.protection_body: If any items are assigned to a shelf, deactivation is blocked automatically. The error message tells you how many items are still linked — reassign them first, then try again. The same protection applies to temperature zones and areas.
help.setup_shelves.tip_1: Keep shelf codes short (2–3 characters) so labels fit neatly on physical shelves.
help.setup_shelves.tip_2: Order areas by walking path (kitchen → backyard) — counts flow naturally.
help.setup_shelves.tip_3: When you add a new shelf, assign items the same day — don't let the backlog grow.
help.setup_shelves.q1: Items are missing from the count screen
help.setup_shelves.a1: The item isn't assigned to any shelf, and has no purchase history either. Assign it in the Items master.
help.setup_shelves.q2: Can I rename a temperature zone later?
help.setup_shelves.a2: Yes. Renaming works, but review the linked areas and items manually to stay consistent.
help.setup_shelves.q3: Can I reuse the same shelf layout across multiple stores?
help.setup_shelves.a3: Currently each store is configured independently. A template feature is planned for a future release.
help.setup_suppliers.title: Using the Supplier Master
help.setup_suppliers.intro: The Supplier Master holds everything about your vendors. Keeping it accurate makes the Order Support screen work properly and prevents order mistakes.
help.setup_suppliers.why_body: Complete supplier information lets the system calculate ordering timing automatically.
help.setup_suppliers.why_1: Delivery dates and order deadlines shown automatically
help.setup_suppliers.why_2: No confusion about how to place an order (phone / fax / email etc.)
help.setup_suppliers.what_fields_intro: The supplier edit screen captures the following.
help.setup_suppliers.field_basic_desc: Supplier code (2-digit), company name, short name. The 2-digit code is also the prefix of item codes for that supplier.
help.setup_suppliers.field_contact_desc: Rep name, rep phone, company phone, fax, email, address — so you can reach them instantly when ordering.
help.setup_suppliers.field_order_method_: Phone / fax / email / web / SNS. Planned use: auto-generate order sheets in the future.
help.setup_suppliers.field_schedule_desc: Per-weekday delivery flag, plus the order deadline (days prior, time of day).
help.setup_suppliers.field_holidays_desc: Supplier-specific closed days. Register year-end / Obon etc. Can also inherit store holidays.
help.setup_suppliers.step1_body: Add a new supplier to the master.
help.setup_suppliers.step1_b: Click "New", then enter company name, rep, contact info.
help.setup_suppliers.step1_c: Choose a unique 2-digit code (e.g. 12, 15, 16) — no duplicates.
help.setup_suppliers.step2_body: Configure which weekdays have deliveries, and the order deadline.
help.setup_suppliers.step2_a: In the supplier edit screen, go to the "Delivery Schedule" section.
help.setup_suppliers.step2_b: Check the weekdays when deliveries arrive.
help.setup_suppliers.step2_d: Pick the order cut-off time in 30-minute increments.
help.setup_suppliers.schedule_example_bo: Deliveries on Mon/Wed/Fri, orders due by 12:00 the day before → check Mon/Wed/Fri, set "1 day prior" and "12:00".
help.setup_suppliers.step3_body: Mark days when this supplier is closed.
help.setup_suppliers.step3_a: In the store calendar, choose the supplier in the holidays section.
help.setup_suppliers.step3_b: Click a date on the calendar to toggle it on/off.
help.setup_suppliers.step3_c: Bulk-add buttons exist for public holidays / year-end / Obon.
help.setup_suppliers.step4_body: Specify which stores work with this supplier.
help.setup_suppliers.step4_b: Open the store edit screen, then the "Suppliers" tab.
help.setup_suppliers.step4_c: Check the suppliers used by this store.
help.setup_suppliers.warn_body: Deleting a supplier breaks the links from existing items and purchase history. For stopping a relationship, prefer "Deactivate".
help.setup_suppliers.flow_to_order: How it flows into Order Support
help.setup_suppliers.flow_to_order_body: The information registered here flows automatically into the Order Support screen.
help.setup_suppliers.flow_1: Delivery schedule → auto-calculated "Next delivery" and "Order deadline"
help.setup_suppliers.flow_2: Holidays → deadlines are brought forward if a closed day intervenes
help.setup_suppliers.flow_3: Order method → shown on the supplier card (future: routes auto-generated order sheet)
help.setup_suppliers.tip_1: Assign supplier codes with gaps (not consecutive) so you have room for future additions.
help.setup_suppliers.tip_2: Rep contact and company contact are separate fields — send orders to the rep first.
help.setup_suppliers.tip_3: Review delivery schedules quarterly to match real-world patterns.
help.setup_suppliers.q1: A supplier isn't appearing in Order Support
help.setup_suppliers.a1: Check the delivery schedule isn't empty and the supplier is linked to the store. Both are required.
help.setup_suppliers.q2: I set holidays, but deadlines aren't updating
help.setup_suppliers.a2: A reload may be needed. Also verify the holidays are in the future.
help.setup_suppliers.q3: Can I change a supplier code later?
help.setup_suppliers.a3: You can, but it may conflict with the 2-digit prefix of existing item codes. Consult support before changing.
help.setup_items.title: Using the Item Master
help.setup_items.intro: The Item Master is the heart of the CMS. Every purchase, inventory count, order-support calculation, and report references items registered here.
help.setup_items.why_body: The quality of the item master determines the accuracy of the whole CMS.
help.setup_items.why_1: Correct classification enables meaningful cost breakdowns
help.setup_items.field_identity_desc: Code (5-digit, auto-generated) and name. Use the name as it appears on your purchase invoices.
help.setup_items.field_classification_de: Category (12 types), process level (raw / semi-processed / finished), temperature zone. These drive report groupings.
help.setup_items.field_units_desc: Purchase unit (e.g. case), inventory unit, minimum order unit.
help.setup_items.field_location_desc: Specified as temp zone → area → shelf. Directly controls the inventory-count order.
help.setup_items.field_est_order_qty_des: Typical quantity to order per cycle. Used by Order Support to detect stock shortages.
help.setup_items.code_rule_body: Item codes are 5 digits, anchored to a supplier.
help.setup_items.categories_body: Choose one of these 12 categories. Dashboard pie charts aggregate by this field.
help.setup_items.process_body: Process level classifies items by their transformation state. Dashboards break down cost by this dimension.
help.setup_items.process_raw_desc: Bought and used as-is (vegetables, meat etc.)
help.setup_items.process_finished_desc: Finished products bought ready-to-serve (bread, beverages etc.)
help.setup_items.step1_body: Register items one at a time.
help.setup_items.step1_b: Click "New" at the top, choose a supplier, and save.
help.setup_items.step1_c: Code is auto-assigned. Fill in name, category, process level, temperature zone.
help.setup_items.step2_body: For tens or hundreds of items at once, use CSV import.
help.setup_items.step2_a: Open the CSV import screen and download the template.
help.setup_items.step2_b: Edit the template in Excel (leave code blank for auto-assignment).
help.setup_items.step2_c: Save as CSV, upload, preview, then import.
help.setup_items.csv_format_hint: CSV columns: code, supplier, name, temp zone, purchase unit, inventory unit, standard inventory unit, minimum purchase unit, internal_flag
help.setup_items.step3_body: Assign each item to its storage location (see "Shelves, Temperature Zones & Areas" for details).
help.setup_items.step3_a: In the item edit screen, pick a temperature zone first.
help.setup_items.step3_b: Available areas filter automatically — pick one.
help.setup_items.step3_c: Available shelves filter next — pick the final location.
help.setup_items.est_qty_body: Estimated Order Qty drives the shortage detection on the Order Support screen — a critical number.
help.setup_items.est_qty_auto_body: A statistical formula derives the optimal value from daily breakfast-served counts.
help.setup_items.est_qty_auto_1: Per-breakfast consumption from 90-day purchase history vs. breakfasts served
help.setup_items.est_qty_auto_2: Cycle consumption (mean + 1.65σ) covers ~95% of stockout risk
help.setup_items.est_qty_manual_body: Enter any number directly on the item edit screen — useful for seasonal items or new menu dishes.
help.setup_items.est_qty_history_body: The bottom of the item edit screen shows the "Stats" block and a history table of every change (when, why, by whom).
help.setup_items.tip_1: Use item names exactly as they appear on supplier invoices — matching during entry becomes effortless.
help.setup_items.tip_2: Pick categories carefully at setup and don't change them later — keeps reports comparable over time.
help.setup_items.tip_3: For discontinued items, use "Deactivate" (not delete) so historical data stays intact.
help.setup_items.q1: CSV import fails with an error
help.setup_items.a1: Most common causes: supplier name typos and column order mismatch. Re-download the template and copy rows in exactly.
help.setup_items.a2: Deactivate the older record — it disappears from the UI. Don't delete, or the purchase history link breaks.
help.setup_items.q2: I registered the same item twice by mistake
help.setup_items.a3: Insufficient purchase history in the past 90 days. Either set it manually, or keep operating and request a re-run.
help.setup_revenue.title: Revenue Estimation Setup
help.setup_revenue.intro: The CMS delivers real-time cost and profit estimates from just two inputs: purchase entry and stock count. This chapter covers the required setup.
help.setup_revenue.why_body: Daily inputs drive these automatic outputs:
help.setup_revenue.why_1: Monthly and period-over-period cost of goods sold
help.setup_revenue.why_2: Cost breakdown by item and category
help.setup_revenue.why_3: FL ratio estimate (food + labor cost vs. revenue)
help.setup_revenue.concept_cogs_desc: Calculated as "opening stock + purchases − closing stock". Consumed materials, not cash flow.
help.setup_revenue.concept_fifo_desc: Older purchases are assumed consumed first — matches real kitchen behavior.
help.setup_revenue.concept_margin_desc: Set a target profit margin to visualize the gap between plan and actual.
help.setup_revenue.formula_body: COGS = Opening stock + Purchases − Closing stock
help.setup_revenue.step1_body: Enter the company's target profit margin.
help.setup_revenue.step1_c: Save. Future reports compare actuals against this target.
help.setup_revenue.step2_title: ② Keep Daily Entries Up to Date
help.setup_revenue.step2_body: Accuracy depends entirely on consistent daily input.
help.setup_revenue.step2_a: Enter purchase invoices in "Purchase Entry" the same day they arrive.
help.setup_revenue.step2_b: Count inventory at month-end, record in "Inventory Count".
help.setup_revenue.step2_c: A beginning-of-month count gives even more accuracy (recommended).
help.setup_revenue.step3_body: Review the estimates on the Report screens.
help.setup_revenue.step3_b: Check monthly cost, share, and year-over-year.
help.setup_revenue.step3_c: Drill down by item or category.
help.setup_revenue.interpret_body: How you interpret the numbers shapes your next actions.
help.setup_revenue.interpret_dashboard_b: The dashboard shows pie-chart share visualizations.
help.setup_revenue.interpret_1: Supplier share: concentration risk (inputs for diversification decisions)
help.setup_revenue.interpret_3: Process-level share: in-house vs. outsourced balance
help.setup_revenue.interpret_dead_stock_: The "Dead Stock (30+ days without delivery)" table shows idle inventory with value. A cue for use-up menus or menu changes.
help.setup_revenue.warn_body: Missing stock counts distort costs dramatically. Always record every item in the month-end count.
help.setup_revenue.data_quality_body: Following these practices significantly improves estimate accuracy.
help.setup_revenue.dq_1: Enter purchase invoices same-day (accurate unit price and qty)
help.setup_revenue.dq_2: Count inventory at least monthly (weekly for high-turnover items)
help.setup_revenue.dq_3: Deactivate unused items to hide them from the UI
help.setup_revenue.dq_4: Set category and process level on every item
help.setup_revenue.tip_1: Counting at both beginning and end of month sharply improves monthly cost accuracy.
help.setup_revenue.tip_2: Glance at the category pie chart monthly to catch mix-change anomalies early.
help.setup_revenue.tip_3: When dead stock value is high, design use-up menus to burn it down.
help.setup_revenue.a1: Most common causes: missing month-end count or a big one-off purchase. Check top suppliers by period in "Purchase Report".
help.setup_revenue.a2: Target margin may not be entered. Check Admin → Profit Settings.
help.setup_revenue.q3: I want to see FIFO calculation details
help.setup_revenue.a3: "Monthly Usage Report" shows per-item consumption and unit-price trends.
